originalTitle: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
title: A Big Happy Welcome Home for Our Cartoon Hero!
content:
Hello, super explorers and curious kids! I'm your friendly news reporter, bringing you a very special and happy story from our amazing continent, Africa!

A Big Happy Welcome Home!

Guess what? Something wonderful has happened! A very special person named Biniam Solomon is finally home! Biniam is an artist from a country in Africa called Eritrea. He is what we call a "cartoonist." Do you know what a cartoonist does?

A cartoonist is like a magic storyteller with a pen! They draw pictures that tell stories, sometimes funny ones, sometimes ones that make you think. Biniam Solomon was very good at drawing these special pictures.

Who is Biniam Solomon?

Biniam Solomon used to draw lots and lots of clever pictures. These pictures were called cartoons. He would draw them for newspapers. His cartoons were super interesting because they helped people understand things happening in their country in a fun, easy way.

Imagine your favorite comic book, but instead of superheroes fighting villains, the pictures talk about everyday life or important ideas. That’s what Biniam did! He made people smile, but he also made them think about things around them.

Did you know? Cartoons have been around for a very long time! Even ancient people drew pictures on walls to tell stories, just like cartoonists do today!

Where is Eritrea?

Eritrea is a beautiful country in East Africa. It has lovely coasts along the Red Sea, with clear blue waters. It's full of interesting history and wonderful people, just like many other countries in Africa.

It’s important to remember that Africa is a very big continent with many different countries, languages, and cultures. Eritrea is one of those special places!

Did you know? Eritrea is home to nine different ethnic groups, each with its own unique traditions and language! Isn't that amazing?

A Long, Long Time Away

Now, here's the part that makes us a little sad, but it has a happy ending! For a very, very long time, Biniam Solomon was not at home. He was in a place called prison. He was in prison for 15 whole years.

Imagine being away from your family, your friends, and your drawing pens for 15 years! That's like waiting for 15 birthdays to pass by, or seeing your little brother or sister grow up from a baby to a big kid!

Why was he there? Well, Biniam drew cartoons that sometimes asked big questions or showed things that some people in charge didn't like. In some places, it's not always easy for people to share all their ideas or stories freely, especially in newspapers.

Long ago, the newspapers and places where people shared news in Eritrea stopped being "free." This means they couldn't always print every story or every cartoon. Because Biniam drew cartoons that made people think a lot, he was taken away.

It's important to know that he was kept in prison without anyone saying exactly what wrong thing he had done. This is called being "held without charge," which means no one ever told him he broke a specific law in court.

The Power of Pictures and Stories

Biniam’s cartoons were special. They were "witty" which means they were clever and funny. They were also "critical," which means they sometimes made people think about how things could be better.

Think about your own drawings. Maybe you draw your family, your pets, or an amazing adventure! Your drawings can make people feel happy, or they can make them think. Biniam's drawings did that for many grown-ups in Eritrea.

Did you know? Drawing and telling stories are super important! They help us share our feelings, our dreams, and our ideas with the world! What kind of stories do you like to tell?

Joyful News: He is FREE!

After all those years, something truly wonderful has happened! Biniam Solomon is finally free! Can you imagine the big smiles, the happy tears, and the huge hugs when he saw his family again?

This news brings so much joy to many people who remembered his amazing cartoons. It reminds us that even after a very long time, good things can happen.

His release means he can finally go home, be with his loved ones, and maybe, just maybe, pick up his pen and draw more wonderful pictures again. We hope so!

This is a big moment for Biniam, for his family, and for everyone who believes in the power of art and stories. It shows that sometimes, even after a long wait, hope can shine very brightly.

Did you know? Hope is a very strong feeling! It helps us believe that good things will happen, even when times are tough. Biniam's story is a story of hope!

What We Can Learn

Biniam’s story teaches us a few important things. It teaches us that art, like drawing cartoons, is a powerful way to share ideas and make people think. It also teaches us about being patient and never giving up hope.

And it reminds us that everyone deserves to be treated fairly and to share their thoughts and stories. It's important for people to be able to express themselves, just like you express yourself when you draw, sing, or tell a story.

So, let's all give a big, warm, imaginary welcome home to Biniam Solomon! We are so happy for him and his family. And remember, keep drawing, keep telling stories, and keep exploring the wonderful world around you!

That's all for now from your kid-friendly news reporter. Stay curious!
summary: A kind cartoonist from Eritrea named Biniam Solomon has been set free after 15 long years, bringing happiness to his family and everyone who loved his clever drawings.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that telling stories and drawing pictures is a special way to share ideas, and it reminds us that everyone deserves to be free and happy.

originalTitle: Death toll from Ethiopia floods and landslides rises to more than 100
title: Big Rains in Ethiopia: Helping Our Friends!
content:
Hello from Ethiopia!

Hello, lovely friends! Your African news reporter is here to tell you about something important. We have some news from a beautiful country in Africa called Ethiopia. Ethiopia is a big place with lots of amazing people and animals. But recently, Ethiopia has had a tough time. There was too, too much rain! Imagine if it rained all day and all night for many, many days. That's what happened in some parts of Ethiopia. When it rains too much, something called a "flood" can happen. It also caused something scary called a "landslide." Many people got hurt, and some even lost their homes and their loved ones. We are very sad to hear this news, and we send our love to everyone there.

What Are Floods?

What is a flood? Let me tell you in a super simple way! A flood happens when there is too, too much water. This water comes from heavy rain, like a giant shower that never stops. The rivers get very, very full, and the water spills out over the land. It covers roads, houses, and even fields where food grows. Imagine your bathtub overflowing! That's a tiny flood in your bathroom. Now imagine that happening outside, everywhere, across big areas! When water covers everything, it makes it hard for people to get around. Houses can get very wet and sometimes even break. It can be very dangerous for people and animals, as the water can move very fast. People have to leave their homes to be safe. **Did you know?** Water is very strong! Even a little bit of fast-moving water can push big things over.

What Are Landslides?

Now, let's talk about something else that happened because of the heavy rain: a "landslide." What is a landslide? It sounds like land is sliding, and that's exactly right! Ethiopia has many hills and tall mountains. When it rains a lot, a lot, the soil on these hills gets super wet. Imagine if you made a sandcastle, and then poured lots and lots of water on it. The sandcastle might start to fall apart and slide down very fast, right? That's what happens with a landslide on a bigger scale. The wet soil, rocks, and sometimes even trees on the side of a hill or mountain suddenly slide down very, very fast. It's like a giant mud slide that rushes down the hill! This can be very dangerous because it can bury houses and roads. It can happen very quickly, so people don't have much time to move away. It makes it hard for people to travel and for help to arrive. **Did you know?** Tree roots are like strong hands that hold the soil together! When there are lots of trees, landslides are less likely to happen because the roots act like a natural glue.

Helping Our Friends!

This is very sad news, and we feel for our friends in Ethiopia. Many people there have lost their homes, their farms, and their precious things. But guess what? People are not giving up! Helpers are working very, very hard to keep everyone safe. They are looking for people who need help in the flooded areas. They are bringing food, clean water, and warm blankets to families who have lost everything. They are helping clear the thick mud and heavy rocks from the landslides. Everyone is trying to be kind and help each other, just like good friends do. That's what we do in Africa, and all over the world, when things are tough. When we see someone sad, we try to make them happy. When someone needs help, we offer our hands and our hearts. It's important to remember that people are very strong and they will rebuild their lives and their communities. **Did you know?** When we help others, it makes us feel good too! Helping is a wonderful and powerful thing to do for everyone.

Why Is This Happening?

You might be wondering, "Why did it rain so, so much in Ethiopia?" Well, grown-ups who study the weather are called "forecasters." A "forecaster" is like a weather detective! They look at the sky, use special tools, and use big computers to guess what the weather will be like. These weather detectives say that the storms are becoming "more intense." What does "more intense" mean? It means the storms are getting bigger and much stronger. They bring even more rain than before and sometimes stronger winds that can blow things over. It's like when you have a little drizzle, but then it turns into a huge, noisy downpour! These super strong storms are happening more often in that part of Africa. The rain is falling much harder and for longer periods of time. **Did you know?** Weather forecasters help us know if we need to bring an umbrella to school or wear a warm jacket to play outside! They keep us safe and prepared.

Our Earth Is Getting Warmer: Global Warming

Now, let's talk about why these storms are getting so big and strong. The weather detectives say it's partly because of something called "global warming." Wow, that's a big phrase! Let me break it down for you. "Global" means the whole Earth, our amazing planet that we all live on. "Warming" means getting hotter, like when you put your hand near a warm stove or a warm mug of hot cocoa. So, global warming means our whole Earth is slowly getting warmer and warmer, little by little. Why is our Earth getting warmer? It's because of things like cars that use fuel, big factories that make things, and burning certain fuels for energy. These things put special gases into the air that we breathe. These gases act like a big, invisible blanket around our Earth, trapping the sun's heat inside. When the Earth gets warmer, it changes the weather all around the world. It can make some places hotter, some places drier, and other places, like Ethiopia, have much bigger storms and much more rain. It's like when you change one part of a big puzzle, all the other parts of the puzzle change too! **Did you know?** We can all help the Earth by saving electricity (turn off lights!), walking or riding bikes instead of using cars, and planting more trees! Every little bit helps our planet.

What Can We Learn?

It's sad to hear about what happened in Ethiopia. But it also teaches us some important lessons that we can all remember. One big lesson is to always be kind and help each other, especially when our friends are in trouble. When our neighbours or friends need help, we reach out our hands and offer our support. Another important lesson is to learn about our amazing planet Earth. We need to take good care of it every single day. By understanding global warming, we can all do our little part to help make things better. Even small actions, like turning off lights when we leave a room or recycling our old plastic bottles, can make a difference. We can also plant trees! Trees are wonderful because they help hold the soil and make the air clean and fresh for us to breathe. Let's send our love and strength to the brave people of Ethiopia. We hope they can rebuild their homes, their schools, and be safe very soon. Remember to always be curious, be kind, and care for our beautiful Earth! That's all from your African news reporter today. See you next time! **Did you know?** Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 countries and many amazing cultures, animals, and beautiful places to explore.
summary: Many people in Ethiopia are sad because heavy rain caused big floods and landslides, but people are helping each other, and we are learning that our Earth is getting warmer.
whyMatters: This is important because it tells us about sad things that happened to our friends in Ethiopia from too much rain. It also helps us learn that we need to take good care of our Earth so big storms don't happen as often, and everyone can stay safe.
